name: Beli
description: Restaurant-focused social app that ranks dining experiences using a comparative, Elo-style ranking system built from a user's own visited-restaurant history and social graph, rather than absolute star ratings.

disruptionMechanisms: Relative/comparative ranking vs. absolute star ratings
attributes.businessModel: Consumer subscription plus B2B restaurant advertising/insights (monetization largely undisclosed; HCP assumption)
attributes.goToMarket: Consumer organic/viral, word-of-mouth and social sharing
attributes.problemStatement: Restaurant discovery on star-rating platforms like Yelp and Google is noisy and not personalized to an individual's taste; Beli replaces absolute ratings with a comparative ranking system built from a user's own visited restaurants.
attributes.productDescription: Beli lets users rank restaurants head-to-head against places they've already visited, building a personal, continuously re-sorted list, and surfaces recommendations based on taste similarity to friends and other users.

founders.name: Judy Thelen
founders.background: Harvard Business School alumna; built the original restaurant-tracking concept as a shared Google Map with Frost before founding Beli.

founders.name: Eliot Frost
founders.background: Harvard Business School alumnus; co-built the original restaurant-tracking concept; "Beli" is derived from his high school nickname.

name: Partiful
description: Mobile-first, SMS-native event invitation and RSVP platform popular with Gen Z and millennial hosts for informal in-person gatherings.

attributes.businessModel: Undisclosed; brand partnerships and premium features are the plausible paths (HCP assumption)
attributes.problemStatement: Coordinating informal gatherings via group texts, email, or Facebook Events is clunky and impersonal; Partiful gives hosts a fast, design-forward, SMS-native invitation and RSVP tool.
attributes.productDescription: Partiful provides customizable event invitations, SMS-based guest communication, group chat, RSVP tracking, and photo sharing built around the arc of a single event.

founders.name: Shreya Murthy
founders.background: Met co-founder Joy Tao while working at Palantir Technologies; led Partiful through early investor skepticism to becoming a widely used Gen Z consumer app.

founders.name: Joy Tao
founders.background: Met co-founder Shreya Murthy while working at Palantir Technologies.

name: Posh
description: Mobile event discovery and ticketing platform for grassroots and community events, offering organizers a vertical-feed discovery experience alongside ticketing, checkout, and analytics tools.

disruptionMechanisms: Algorithmic discovery feed vs. static listings
Grassroots/community event focus vs. major-venue incumbents
attributes.businessModel: Marketplace / ticketing commission and processing fees
attributes.goToMarket: Organizer-led supply acquisition plus consumer viral discovery
attributes.problemStatement: Small, grassroots events are hard to discover and monetize through legacy ticketing platforms built for large venues; Posh gives independent organizers a TikTok-style discovery feed plus ticketing tools.
attributes.productDescription: Posh combines a vertical-scroll event discovery feed with ticketing, checkout, and organizer analytics, focused on grassroots and community events rather than major-venue shows.

founders.name: Avante Price
founders.background: Grew up DJing in Chicago and competed in hip-hop turntable competitions before dropping out of NYU to co-found Posh.

founders.name: Eli Taylor-Lemire
founders.background: Previously ran a freelance photo/video agency producing content for major fashion publications and musicians; dropped out of NYU to co-found Posh.

name: Pickle
description: Peer-to-peer fashion rental marketplace where clothing supply comes directly from individual lenders' closets rather than company-owned inventory.

attributes.goToMarket: Consumer organic/viral plus influencer and community-led supply acquisition
attributes.problemStatement: Owned-inventory rental platforms carry heavy warehousing and depreciation costs and a limited catalog; Pickle sources rental inventory directly from people's closets, removing inventory risk while offering a larger, more dynamic catalog.
attributes.productDescription: Pickle is a marketplace connecting people who want to rent clothing with people willing to lend items from their own closets, charging a service fee per transaction and offering same-day courier delivery in select markets.

founders.name: Brian McMahon
founders.background: Former Blackstone analyst; co-founded Pickle after observing organic peer-to-peer clothing-sharing behavior.

founders.name: Julia O'Mara
founders.background: Former Blackstone analyst; previously worked with McMahon at Blackstone before co-founding Pickle.

name: Pie
description: IRL friendship app that uses an AI personality quiz to assemble small groups of compatible strangers for in-person events, and pays local organizers to run those events.

disruptionMechanisms: AI-curated small groups vs. open RSVP meetups
Paid event organizers to guarantee quality
attributes.businessModel: Undisclosed; likely future membership/subscription (HCP assumption); current model pays organizers, a net cost
attributes.goToMarket: Local city-by-city launch with paid event organizers and community seeding
attributes.operationalModel: Pure software with a paid-operations layer (compensates human event hosts)
attributes.problemStatement: Existing meetup and dating apps are optimized for browsing profiles, not for reliably getting people into real rooms with compatible strangers; Pie uses an AI personality quiz to assemble small in-person groups and pays organizers to ensure events happen.
attributes.productDescription: Pie matches users into small groups of roughly six compatible people via an AI personality quiz and pays local organizers to host the resulting in-person events.

founders.name: Andy Dunn
founders.background: Co-founded menswear brand Bonobos and led it as CEO through its 2017 acquisition by Walmart.

founders.name: Samir Mahafzah
founders.background: Built a tool called Sparked Connections with Sam Stubbs that was acqui-hired into Pie.

founders.name: Sam Stubbs
founders.background: Co-built Sparked Connections with Samir Mahafzah prior to joining Pie.

name: Timeleft
description: Social platform that matches small groups of strangers, based on a compatibility questionnaire, for a recurring weekly dinner, scaled to 200+ cities and 52 countries.

productCategoryPath: Scheduled group dining and social matching
disruptionMechanisms: Recurring scheduled ritual vs. one-off meetups
attributes.goToMarket: City-by-city consumer launch, viral/word-of-mouth and press-driven
attributes.operationalModel: Pure software with local restaurant-partner operations
attributes.problemStatement: Adult loneliness and the difficulty of making new friends in a new city are growing problems that dating and networking apps do not solve; Timeleft matches small groups of strangers for a recurring weekly dinner ritual.
attributes.productDescription: Timeleft matches five to six compatible strangers via questionnaire for a recurring Wednesday dinner at a partner restaurant, scaled to over 200 cities across 52 countries.

founders.name: Maxime Barbier
founders.background: Serial entrepreneur with prior ventures spanning nightlife promotion and media before founding Timeleft in France.

founders.name: Adrien de Oliveira
founders.background: Named alongside Barbier as co-founder in company-adjacent reporting; current operating role to be confirmed in diligence.

name: Chowdeck
description: On-demand food, grocery, and pharmacy delivery platform operating an owned rider network across 11 cities in Nigeria and Ghana, reporting profitability while scaling.

disruptionMechanisms: Organized on-demand delivery vs. informal/no delivery infrastructure
Bicycle-heavy dense-area logistics for cost efficiency
attributes.businessModel: Marketplace / delivery and commission fees plus subscription (Chowpass) and advertising
attributes.problemStatement: Food and grocery delivery in Nigeria and Ghana has historically been informal, slow, and unreliable; Chowdeck built an organized rider network and dispatch system that delivers in about 30 minutes while remaining profitable in a notoriously low-margin market.
attributes.productDescription: Chowdeck operates a multi-sided delivery platform spanning restaurant food delivery, groceries, pharmacy items, a vendor marketplace (Chowstore), advertising, a subscription tier (Chowpass), and package relay, backed by an owned rider network of over 20,000 riders.

founders.name: Femi Aluko
founders.background: Founded Chowdeck in October 2021 in Lagos; led the company through Y Combinator and multiple funding rounds while reportedly maintaining profitability.

founders.name: Olumide Ojo
founders.background: Named as a founding member alongside Aluko in TechCrunch's coverage of the Series A.

founders.name: Lanre Yusuf
founders.background: Named as a founding member alongside Aluko and Ojo; current operating role to be confirmed in diligence.

name: Bezel
description: Technology-enabled marketplace for buying, selling, and trading pre-owned luxury watches, centered on in-house physical authentication of every listed timepiece.

disruptionMechanisms: Centralized in-house authentication vs. fragmented dealer/grey-market trust problem
Technology-forward UX vs. legacy watch dealers
attributes.goToMarket: Consumer organic plus celebrity/influencer investor network
attributes.operationalModel: Marketplace with physical-goods logistics (in-house authentication, shipping)
attributes.problemStatement: Buying and selling a luxury watch peer-to-peer or through a dealer carries high fraud, counterfeit, and trust risk; Bezel centralizes authentication in-house and gives buyers and sellers price transparency across a large listed inventory.
attributes.productDescription: Bezel lists thousands of pre-owned luxury watches (reported over $500m in inventory value), inspecting each one in-house before delivery and issuing a digital and physical certificate of authenticity, alongside wishlist, price-tracking, and concierge-sourcing tools.

founders.name: Quaid Walker
founders.background: Former product/design employee at Google and watch collector; founded Bezel after a frustrating personal Rolex-buying experience.

founders.name: Chase Payan
founders.background: Childhood friend of Walker's with a finance and operations background; one source spells the name "Chase Pion" — confirm in diligence.

founders.name: Darryl Johnson
founders.background: Named in a single Forbes profile as an engineer and prior Google employee; not corroborated elsewhere in HCP's research.

name: Yope
description: Private, feed-less photo-sharing app for small trusted friend groups, positioned against public algorithm-optimized social feeds; batch substitute for Flagship, which shut down its core creator-storefront product.

technicalApproaches: No public feed/algorithmic ranking by design
disruptionMechanisms: No likes/algorithm as an anti-Instagram positioning
Network effects within small trusted groups
attributes.businessModel: Undisclosed; no monetization confirmed publicly (HCP assumption of future subscription/brand features)
attributes.problemStatement: Public social feeds optimized for likes and performance have made platforms like Instagram feel inauthentic for sharing with close friends; Yope offers a private, feed-less, algorithm-free space for small friend groups to share photos.
attributes.productDescription: Yope lets users send photos to a private friend group, which are assembled into a machine-learning-generated collage rather than a chronological or algorithmic feed, with no likes and no public feed by design.

founders.name: Bahram Ismailau
founders.background: Met co-founder Paul Rudkouski studying together at Belarusian State University; the pair worked on prior products for years before Yope.

founders.name: Paul Rudkouski
founders.background: Met co-founder Bahram Ismailau studying together at Belarusian State University.

description: AI-driven platform that matches strangers by personality assessment and books them into curated in-person experiences such as dinners and comedy shows, aiming to combat social isolation.

technicalApproaches: Curated in-person experience booking (dinners, comedy shows)
disruptionMechanisms: Algorithmic matching to curated real-world events vs. profile-swiping dating/social apps
attributes.businessModel: Undisclosed; likely per-event or subscription fee (HCP assumption)
attributes.goToMarket: City-by-city consumer launch (Los Angeles then New York), press and community-led
attributes.operationalModel: Pure software with local venue/event operations
attributes.problemStatement: Loneliness and social isolation are rising, and existing dating and networking apps optimize for browsing rather than real-world connection; 222 uses a personality assessment to algorithmically match small groups for curated in-person experiences.
attributes.productDescription: 222 matches users via a personality assessment into small groups for booked, real-world experiences (dinners, comedy shows) at local venues, without profiles, DMs, or swiping.

founders.name: Danial Hashemi
founders.background: One of three co-founders of 222, founded in 2023; identified in Fast Company's coverage of the Series A.

founders.name: Keyan Kazemian
founders.background: One of three co-founders of 222, founded in 2023.

founders.name: Arman Roshannai
founders.background: One of three co-founders of 222, founded in 2023.

geography: United States (New York City, Los Angeles)
